title: 0.1% Cresyl Violet Stain (pH 4.3)
author: Shaina Robbins
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wcjfaun

steps.title: Measure ddH2O
steps.instruction: Measure 500 mL of ddH2O or molecular grade H2O and pour into a glass beaker

steps.title: Add Cresyl Violet Acetate
steps.instruction: Aliquot 0.5 g of cresyl violet acetate into the glass beaker from the above step Cresyl Violet acetate pure high purity biological stain Contributed by users Catalog #AC229630050 Causes skin irritation May cause respiratory irritation Causes serious eye irritation

steps.title: Dissolve Cresyl Violet
steps.instruction: Put a magnetic stir bar into the glass beaker (containing H2O and cresyl violet acetate) and place on a hot plate at 100 °C and stir until cresyl violet is dissolved Allow solution to come to room temperature once cresyl violet is dissolved

steps.title: Adjust pH
steps.instruction: Test the pH of the solution. Add glacial acetic acid dropwise until the desired (pH 4.3) is achieved Acetic Acid Glacial Contributed by users Catalog #A38-212

steps.title: Filter and Store
steps.instruction: Pour the contents of the 0.1% Cresyl Violet solution through a sterile membrane filter into a light sensitive jar and close lid
